examples.title: Deadlock Demonstration - Two Processes
examples.description: Demonstrates a simple deadlock scenario with two processes and two resources
examples.code:
// DEADLOCK DEMONSTRATION
SHARED resources: R1, R2

PROCESS P1:
BEGIN
    WHILE true DO
        ACQUIRE(R1)
        PRINT "P1 acquired R1"
        ACQUIRE(R2)
        PRINT "P1 acquired R2"
        RELEASE(R1)
        RELEASE(R2)
    END WHILE
END

PROCESS P2:
BEGIN
    WHILE true DO
        ACQUIRE(R2)
        PRINT "P2 acquired R2"
        ACQUIRE(R1)
        PRINT "P2 acquired R1"
        RELEASE(R2)
        RELEASE(R1)
    END WHILE
END

// If P1 acquires R1 and P2 acquires R2 simultaneously,
// both processes wait forever for the other resource - DEADLOCK
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates deadlock when two processes acquire resources in different order. P1 acquires R1 then R2, while P2 acquires R2 then R1. If both run concurrently and each acquires their first resource, they wait forever for the second resource held by the other process.

examples.title: Resource Allocation Graph Cycle Detection
examples.description: Python implementation to detect cycle in resource allocation graph
examples.explanation: This Python code implements Resource Allocation Graph cycle detection. It builds a directed graph from assignment and request edges, then uses DFS to detect cycles. If a cycle exists with single-instance resources, deadlock is guaranteed.
examples.timeComplexity: O(V + E) where V is vertices (processes + resources), E is edges
examples.spaceComplexity: O(V + E) for adjacency list storage

examples.title: Banker's Algorithm - Deadlock Avoidance
examples.description: C program implementing Banker's Algorithm for deadlock avoidance
examples.explanation: Banker's Algorithm is a deadlock avoidance algorithm that ensures the system always remains in a safe state. Before allocating resources, it simulates the allocation and checks if a safe sequence of process execution exists. If not, the request is denied.
